questions.text: The Babylonian number system is based on which base?
questions.explanation: The ancient Babylonians used a sexagesimal (base-60) number system.

questions.text: The Mayan number system is based on which base?
questions.explanation: The ancient Mayans used a vigesimal (base-20) number system.

questions.text: The decimal (Hindu-Arabic) number system is based on which base?
questions.explanation: The Hindu-Arabic number system that we use today is base-10 (decimal).

questions.text: The binary number system uses only digits:
questions.explanation: Binary (base-2) uses only two digits: 0 and 1.

questions.text: Which Roman numeral represents 4?
questions.explanation: In Roman numerals, IV = 5 − 1 = 4. The subtractive notation is used.

questions.text: What does the Roman numeral XLVII represent?
Natural numbers start from:
questions.explanation: Natural numbers are {1, 2, 3, 4, ...}. They start from 1.

questions.text: Whole numbers include:
questions.explanation: Whole numbers = {0, 1, 2, 3, ...} = 0 along with all natural numbers.

questions.text: Which of the following is an integer but NOT a natural number?
questions.explanation: Integers include ..., −3, −2, −1, 0, 1, 2, 3, ... Both 0 and −3 are integers but not natural numbers.

questions.text: A rational number is a number that can be written as p/q where:

options.a: p and q are integers, q ≠ 0
options.b: p and q are natural numbers

questions.explanation: A rational number is of the form p/q, where p and q are integers and q ≠ 0.

questions.text: Which of the following is an irrational number?
questions.explanation: √2 cannot be expressed as p/q (an exact fraction). Its decimal expansion never terminates or repeats.

questions.text: Which of the following sets of numbers is correctly ordered from smallest to largest?
questions.explanation: Natural ⊂ Whole ⊂ Integers ⊂ Rational ⊂ Real. Each set contains the previous one.

questions.text: Every natural number is:
questions.explanation: Natural numbers are {1, 2, 3, ...}. Whole numbers are {0, 1, 2, 3, ...}. Every natural number is a whole number.

questions.text: The number π (pi) is:
questions.explanation: π = 3.14159... is a non-terminating, non-repeating decimal. It is an irrational number.

questions.text: The number 0 belongs to which of the following sets?

options.c: Whole numbers, integers and rational numbers

questions.explanation: 0 is a whole number (W), an integer (Z), and a rational number (0 = 0/1). It is not a natural number.

questions.text: Which is the Roman numeral for 90?
questions.explanation: XC = 100 − 10 = 90. Roman numerals use subtractive notation.

questions.text: The decimal number 13 in binary is:
Which ancient civilisation is credited with introducing zero as a numeral?
questions.explanation: The concept of zero as a numeral and placeholder was developed and formalised in ancient India.

questions.text: The binary equivalent of the decimal number 8 is:
questions.explanation: 8 = 2³ = 1000 in binary.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a real number?
questions.explanation: √−9 is an imaginary number, not a real number. It cannot be placed on the real number line.

questions.text: Convert the Roman numeral MCMXCIX to decimal.
What is the decimal number 25 written in binary?
Write the decimal number 2026 in Roman numerals.
The binary number 1010 equals which decimal number?
Which of the following is a rational number between √2 and √3?
questions.explanation: √2 ≈ 1.414 and √3 ≈ 1.732. Both 1.5 and 1.3 lie in this range. Both are rational (terminating decimals).

questions.text: The sum of three consecutive even numbers is 78. What is the smallest even number?
questions.explanation: Let the numbers be n, n+2, n+4. Sum = 3n + 6 = 78. 3n = 72. n = 24.

questions.text: In base 5, how would you write the decimal number 17?
questions.explanation: 17 = 3×5 + 2 = 32 in base 5. (3 fives and 2 ones.)

questions.text: The binary number 11111 equals which decimal number?
Which of these is NOT an example of an irrational number?
questions.explanation: √36 = 6, which is a rational integer. The others (√5, π, ∛2) are irrational.

questions.text: The Roman numeral for 1776 is:
questions.explanation: 1776 = 1000 + 700 + 70 + 6 = M + DCC + LXX + VI = MDCCLXXVI.

questions.text: The sum of two binary numbers 1101 and 1011 in decimal is:
Between which two consecutive integers does √50 lie?
questions.explanation: 7² = 49 and 8² = 64. Since 49 < 50 < 64, √50 lies between 7 and 8.

questions.text: The Babylonian number system writing uses groups of symbols. 60 minutes in an hour comes from which ancient base system?
questions.explanation: Our system of 60 seconds in a minute and 60 minutes in an hour comes from the Babylonian base-60 system.

questions.text: Is 0.333... (recurring) a rational or irrational number? Why?
questions.explanation: 0.333... = 1/3, which is of the form p/q. Recurring (repeating) decimals are always rational numbers.

questions.text: In which number system does the number 1024 have a special significance?
questions.explanation: 1024 = 2¹⁰. It is a power of 2, making it especially significant in the binary (base-2) number system used in computers.

questions.text: How many digits are needed to write the number 100 in base 2 (binary)?
questions.explanation: 100 in binary = 1100100. Let's verify: 64+32+4 = 100. That's 7 digits (1100100).

questions.text: Convert CDXLVIII (Roman) to decimal.
Add the binary numbers 10110 and 01101.
How many rational numbers lie between 1/4 and 1/2?
questions.explanation: Between any two distinct rational numbers, there are infinitely many rational numbers.

questions.text: Write 2024 in Roman numerals.
What is the decimal equivalent of the binary number 110101?
If today is the year 2026, the Roman numeral for this year is:
Which of these decimals is irrational?
questions.explanation: A non-terminating, non-recurring decimal is irrational. 0.101001000... has no repeating pattern, so it is irrational.

questions.text: How many real numbers are there between 0 and 1?
questions.explanation: The real number line is continuous. There are infinitely many real numbers between any two distinct real numbers.

questions.text: The product of a binary number 11 (in binary) and its decimal equivalent is:
questions.explanation: Binary 11 = decimal 3. Product = 3 × 3 = 9.

questions.text: Categorise the following — π, −7, 0, √2, 5/3. How many are rational numbers?
questions.explanation: Rational: −7 (= −7/1), 0 (= 0/1), 5/3. Irrational: π, √2. Total rational = 3.

cases.case_text: Meera is helping her school celebrate its 125th anniversary. The principal asks students to write the year in different number systems. The school was founded in the year 1899 CE. The current year for the anniversary is 2024 CE.

subparts.text: Write the founding year 1899 in Roman numerals.
Write the anniversary year 2024 in Roman numerals.
Express the number of years (125) in binary.

cases.case_text: Ravi is learning about number systems in his science class. He reads that computers use binary, that ancient astronomers used base-60, and that modern mathematics classifies all numbers into sets. He makes a chart to compare different types of numbers.

subparts.text: Ravi finds that the number 7/0 cannot be classified. Why?

options.c: It equals infinity and is real

subparts.explanation: Division by zero is undefined in mathematics. 7/0 has no value and cannot be classified as any type of number.

subparts.text: Ravi charts: Natural ⊂ Whole ⊂ Integers ⊂ Rational ⊂ Real. Which number belongs to Integers but NOT to Whole numbers?
subparts.explanation: −4 is an integer but not a whole number (whole numbers are 0, 1, 2, 3, ...).

subparts.text: In the binary system, what is 15 + 1?
subparts.explanation: 15 in decimal = 1111 in binary. Adding 1: 1111 + 1 = 10000 in binary = 16 in decimal. So the answer is 10000 in binary.
